seoTitle: How to Increase Your PayPal Sending Limit (2026)
metaDescription: See how to lift PayPal's sending and withdrawal caps by linking a bank account, verifying your identity, and submitting compliance documents.
intro: Hitting a sending or withdrawal cap on PayPal can interrupt your business right when you need funds most. This guide shows how to check your current limits and complete the verification steps needed to unlock higher thresholds. It's built for sellers and regular users who keep bumping into transaction caps.

steps.heading: Check your current limits
steps.body: Go to Settings and open View Limits to see your active yearly sending and monthly withdrawal caps.

steps.heading: Start the verification process
steps.body: Click Lift Limits or Verify to begin the account expansion steps PayPal requires.

steps.heading: Link and confirm a bank account
steps.body: Add your bank routing details, then return a few days later to enter the small micro-deposit amounts PayPal sent to confirm ownership.

steps.heading: Upload identity documents
steps.body: Submit a clear photo of your government ID or passport, plus proof of address or tax registration if requested.

steps.heading: Submit and monitor your status
steps.body: Send your verification package for review and watch your account status update to fully verified with expanded limits.

faq.question: How long does verification take?
faq.answer: Micro-deposit confirmation typically takes a couple of days to appear, and document review can take a few more business days after that.

faq.question: Do business accounts get higher limits automatically?
faq.answer: Verified business accounts generally have access to higher commercial transaction thresholds than unverified personal accounts.

faq.question: What happens if I skip identity verification?
faq.answer: Your account stays capped at entry-level limits and may face additional restrictions on sending or withdrawing funds.

faq.question: Can inconsistent sales patterns trigger new holds even after I'm verified?
faq.answer: Yes, sudden spikes or unusual activity can still trigger automated security reviews even on verified accounts.
